scenario: Beacon Insurance handles 4000 claims per day in Camunda 8.8. After a rare data corruption, one instance is stuck on an obsolete user task and must be moved forward to the verify-claim service task without redeploying the process or cancelling the instance.
question: Which Operate capability moves the active token from the current activity to a different activity in the same definition?

options.text: Process instance modification in Operate cancels the active token on the current activity and creates a new token on the chosen target activity
Process instance modification in Operate redeploys the process definition with the new target activity marked as the start of the modified flow
Process instance migration in Operate moves the token to a different activity within the same process definition version on the running instance
Process instance migration in Operate cancels and recreates the instance starting at the new activity while preserving the original business key

a.text: Correct. The Operate docs describe instance modification as cancelling tokens on the current activity and creating new tokens on the operator-selected target activity.

b.text: Incorrect. Modification does not redeploy the process definition; it only changes the active token in the running instance.

c.text: Incorrect. Migration moves an instance between two process definitions or versions, not between activities of the same definition.

d.text: Incorrect. Migration does not cancel and recreate the instance; that is the role of instance modification for a single instance.

explanation: Instance modification in Operate cancels and creates tokens on the running instance, allowing operators to recover from a stuck activity without redeployment.
